title: QUIC Interop Retry Child-Process Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests, build execution, and artifact inspection.
preconditions: REQ-QUIC-INT-0007, REQ-QUIC-INT-0008, REQ-QUIC-INT-0010, REQ-QUIC-INT-0011, REQ-QUIC-API-0002, REQ-QUIC-API-0004, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0121, and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0122 remain green.
The harness can dispatch `retry` through the managed child-process path.
Loopback UDP sockets and the test TLS material are available.
scope: Verify the managed child-process `retry` testcase, the one-valid-Retry emission gate, the client replay, the replayed Initial acceptance, and exit-code honesty only after post-Retry handshake completion.
procedure: Run the new retry requirement-home test and confirm the server issues one Retry while the client observes exactly one Retry transition and the handshake completes on the replayed path.
Run the current interop and CRT guard tests that protect the supported floor.
Render and validate the SpecTrace JSON and Markdown artifacts after any canonical JSON updates.
expected_result: The child-process harness proves exactly one valid Retry on the real managed path, the client replays exactly once, the server accepts the replayed Initial, and exit code 0 is returned only after post-Retry handshake completion. Unsupported testcases continue to return `127`.
status_summary: Landed; this verification slice records the proof contract for the managed child-process retry contract.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: Public API widening, IsSupported widening, transfer enablement, multi-Retry support, broader token lifecycle or NEW_TOKEN behavior, broader TLS policy, trust-store policy, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, and key update remain intentionally deferred.
